front: Why is a touchscreen both an input and an output device?
back: It displays information to the user and also detects where the user touches the screen.

front: What are the three touchscreen technologies in the specification?
How does a resistive touchscreen work?
back: It uses two conductive layers separated by an insulating layer. When pressed, the layers make contact and the position is calculated.

front: How does a capacitive touchscreen work?
back: It uses conductive layers on glass. Touching the screen changes the electric field and the position is calculated.

front: How does an infra-red touchscreen work?
back: It uses a grid of infra-red beams. Touching the screen interrupts the beams and the position is calculated.

front: What is one advantage of a resistive touchscreen?
back: It is cheaper and can be used with gloves.

front: What is one disadvantage of a resistive touchscreen?
back: It is less accurate and usually only detects a single touch.

front: What is one advantage of a capacitive touchscreen?
back: It is more accurate and supports multi-touch.

front: What is one disadvantage of a capacitive touchscreen?
back: It is more expensive and usually does not work with gloves.

front: Why might a resistive touchscreen be chosen instead of a capacitive one?
back: Because it is cheaper and can be used with gloves or a stylus.
